content.full_text:
MEYERS, NAVE, RIBACK & SILVER
MICHAEL R. NAVE
STEVEN R. MEYERS
ELIZABETH H. SILVER
MICHAEL S. RIBACK
CLIFFORD F. CAMPBELL
MICHAEL F. RODRIQUEZ
KATHLEEN FAUBION
FREDERICK S. ETHERIDGE
WENDY A. ROBERTS
DAVID W. SKINNER
STEVEN T. MATIAS

A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION

PENINSULA OFFICE

GATEWAY PLAZA
Dec 23
'92
777 DAVIS STREET, SUITE 200
SAN LEANDRO, CALIFORNIA 94577
TELEPHONE: (510) 351-4300
FACSIMILE: (510) 351-4481

1220 HOLLENBECK AVENUE
SUNNYVALE, CA 94087-3251
TELEPHONE: (408) 738-7511
FACSIMILE: (408) 738-7566

December 22, 1992

SONOMA COUNTY
(707) 546-3126

OF COUNSEL
ANDREEA J. SALTZMAN

REPLY TO

San Leandro

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95812

RE: Request for Advice Letter

Dear Hallabrin:

This office serves as the City Attorney for the City of
Dublin. Newly elected City Councilmember Houston has requested
an advice letter as set forth below.

BACKGROUND

Guy Houston is a newly elected councilmember in the City of
Dublin. The City Council is scheduled to consider a general plan
amendment and specific plan for the Eastern Dublin area (the "GPA
Decision") on January 12, 1993. The area encompassed in the GPA
Decision is approximately 7,000 acres. As presently drafted, the
GPA would provide for development of 18,000 new dwelling units,
with complete buildout over a 20 to 30 year period. The General
Plan Amendment and Specific Plan for the Eastern Dublin area is
sponsored by the City, not a developer. There are approximately
50 property owners in the 7,000 acre area.

Councilmember Houston is a limited partner in Valley
Capital, a mortgage brokering business. Virtually all of Valley
Capital's business concerns existing homes, not new homes.
Councilmember Houston has only generated one mortgage on a new
home which was not located in Dublin. Approximately 5% of Valley
Capital's business is within the City of Dublin, with the
remaining 95% of its business being conducted outside the City's
boundaries. More specifically, approximately 12% of
Councilmember Houston's business is within the City of Dublin,
with the remainder conducted outside the City. Finally,
approximately 5% of Valley's business involves "jumbo" loans

Scott Hallabrin
December 22, 1992
Page 2


(loans over $202,300 in amount), and the average loan amount of
Valley's business is $160,000.

Valley Capital receives a commission for mortgages which it
brokers. Commissions are measured as 1% to 1½% of the loan
amount. As a limited partner, Councilmember Houston receives 80%
of those commissions which he generates, with the remaining 20%
going to the business.

ISSUES

May Councilmember Houston participate in the City Council
GPA Decision on January 12, 1993?

We believe uncertainty exists as to any foreseeable
financial effect, given the fact that the GPA Decision will
result in the construction of new homes, while Valley Capital's
business focuses almost exclusively on brokering mortgages for
existing, not new, homes. Furthermore, only a very small
percentage of Valley Capital and Councilmember Houston's business
is conducted within the city limits. We believe there are also
issues concerning material financial effects of the GPA Decision.

As mentioned above, the Council has scheduled the GPA
Decision for its January 12, 1993 Council meeting. We would
appreciate resolution of this issue prior to this date. If you
have any questions concerning this or need additional information, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,

MEYERS, NAVE, RIBACK & SILVER

[signature]

Elizabeth H. Silver
City Attorney
City of Dublin

EHS:dsp
cc: Guy Houston, Councilmember

sections.question_synthetic: Can Councilmember Houston, a limited partner in a mortgage brokering business that primarily handles existing home loans with minimal activity in Dublin, participate in voting on a general plan amendment for development of 18,000 new dwelling units in Eastern Dublin?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from city attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Contains background section and issue/question section but no conclusion section from FPPC. Document seeks advance ruling before January 12, 1993 council meeting.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney for Dublin requests an FPPC advice letter regarding whether newly elected Councilmember Houston may participate in voting on a general plan amendment for Eastern Dublin, given his financial interest as a limited partner in Valley Capital, a mortgage brokering business.
